fallback_questions: Which audience and business process owns this dashboard?
Is the dashboard an overview, self-service view, object-management page, alert/subscription, analytical tool, experiment report, or project/status dashboard?
Which action should the user take after reading it?
Which metrics, cuts, source freshness, and data quality caveats are accepted?
Which selectors, object relations, and navigation targets must be preserved?

required_questions: Who owns the dashboard product and the support or feedback channel?
Is Usage Analytics enabled, disabled, or privacy-limited for this dashboard?
Which adoption metric is reviewed, and on what cadence?
Which performance, error, and data-freshness thresholds trigger action?
What are the promotion, deprecation, and retirement rules?

overview.selection_reason: Use when leaders or process owners need compact business state and a clear next place to inspect.
overview.audience: executives, process owners, and managers who need a fast status read
overview.job_to_be_done: See the current state, identify the largest exception or opportunity, and navigate to the right detail page.
required_inputs_questions: Which five to seven primary metrics define business state?
Which period, explicit comparator if any, and source freshness must be visible?
Which detail dashboards or object pages are linked from this hub?
Which global selectors are safe without hiding required context?
overview.recommended_layout: one screen or compact tab: KPI row, one trend, one ranked comparison, optional map only when geography is part of the decision, then navigation links
overview.selector_filter_behavior: few global filters with visible defaults and reset; avoid selector-heavy exploration
overview.navigation_relations: hub links to self-service, object-management, or alert pages; selectors target only declared charts
overview.expected_interactivity: tooltips for metric definitions, drill links to detail, optional cross-filtering only when relation contract is explicit

self_service.selection_reason: Use when validated users need to answer related questions without requesting new dashboard variants.
self_service.audience: business users and analysts exploring validated data
self_service.job_to_be_done: Slice trusted data, inspect exact values, and reuse the same dashboard for many related questions.
required_inputs_questions: Which fields and metrics are approved for self-service?
Which filters need defaults, reset behavior, and dependencies?
What detail table or export path is expected?
Which data quality caveats and freshness notes must stay visible?
self_service.recommended_layout: selector band or side panel, summary strip, comparison/trend blocks, detail table with stable sort and source notes
self_service.selector_filter_behavior: rich filters with defaults, reset, dependencies, and documented affected widgets
self_service.navigation_relations: selectors drive declared chart/table targets; detail rows can link to object pages
self_service.expected_interactivity: filtering, sorting, table pagination, tooltips, and optional row-to-detail navigation
anti_patterns: selectors that silently affect unrelated widgets

object_management.selection_reason: Use when the dashboard is a working page for managing entities, statuses, owners, and reasons.
object_management.audience: operators and managers responsible for object lifecycle
object_management.job_to_be_done: Find objects needing action, understand why status changed, and move to the right owner or detail.
required_inputs_questions: Which business object and lifecycle states are in scope?
Which reasons, owners, and next actions must be visible?
Which object identifiers and relations are needed for navigation?
Which status freshness and source reliability rules apply?
object_management.recommended_layout: status summary, action queue table, reason breakdown, lifecycle trend, object-detail navigation
object_management.selector_filter_behavior: object/status/owner filters with relation targets and no hidden default that removes urgent items
object_management.navigation_relations: overview-to-object and queue-to-detail links are mandatory; selector targets are explicit
object_management.expected_interactivity: sorting by urgency, filtering by owner/status, row links to object detail, tooltips for state definitions

alerts_mailing.selection_reason: Use when the dashboard must surface exceptions for action, often through a subscription or compact message.
alerts_mailing.audience: operators and accountable owners who need exception visibility
alerts_mailing.job_to_be_done: Notice threshold breaches quickly, identify the owner, and take the next action.
required_inputs_questions: Which thresholds and severities are accepted?
Who owns each exception and what action is expected?
Which subscription cadence and payload length are usable?
Which false-positive or data freshness caveats must be shown?
alerts_mailing.recommended_layout: compact KPI/threshold strip, exception table, owner/action block, subscription-safe summary
alerts_mailing.selector_filter_behavior: minimal filters with safe defaults; subscriptions should not require manual filter setup
alerts_mailing.navigation_relations: exception rows link to owning process or object detail; alert source links to methodology
alerts_mailing.expected_interactivity: threshold tooltip, owner/action link, optional severity filter
anti_patterns: too many filters for a subscription

analytical_tool.selection_reason: Use when expert users need a method-driven workspace for known analytical questions.
analytical_tool.job_to_be_done: Explore a bounded question space, compare hypotheses, and inspect supporting detail.
required_inputs_questions: Which analytical method or question space is allowed?
Which filters are mandatory and which can be exploratory?
Which metric definitions and caveats must appear near the method?
Which outputs become decisions versus investigation notes?
analytical_tool.recommended_layout: method note, filter workspace, primary relationship/distribution/trend visual, supporting table
analytical_tool.selector_filter_behavior: rich filters with reset and visible active state; avoid filters that invalidate the method
analytical_tool.navigation_relations: method notes link to sources; selected rows or points can navigate to detail when declared
analytical_tool.expected_interactivity: filtering, hover detail, comparison switching, drill to supporting detail
anti_patterns: visual complexity without a defined question

experiment_report.selection_reason: Use when stakeholders must decide whether to continue, stop, or roll out an experiment.
experiment_report.audience: product owners, analysts, and experiment stakeholders
experiment_report.job_to_be_done: Compare hypothesis, cohorts, results, risks, and decision in one narrative report.
required_inputs_questions: What is the hypothesis and decision deadline?
Which cohorts, variants, metrics, and guardrails are accepted?
What baseline, period, and significance or business threshold is meaningful?
What rollout, stop, or follow-up action is expected?
experiment_report.recommended_layout: narrative canvas: hypothesis, cohorts, KPI deltas, trend context, metric table, final decision block
experiment_report.selector_filter_behavior: cohort, variant, and period filters only when they preserve the experiment contract
experiment_report.navigation_relations: methodology and source notes link to detail; final decision links to owner/action
experiment_report.expected_interactivity: cohort/period filtering, metric tooltip definitions, drill to detailed metric table

project_ad_hoc.selection_reason: Use when a bounded project or temporary initiative needs status, risks, owners, and expiry.
project_ad_hoc.audience: project stakeholders, owners, and temporary working groups
project_ad_hoc.job_to_be_done: Track bounded work, risks, blockers, decisions, and next actions until the dashboard expires or becomes permanent.
required_inputs_questions: Who owns the project/status dashboard and when does it expire?
Which milestones, risks, blockers, and actions are tracked?
Which metrics distinguish progress from activity?
Which open questions must stay visible?
project_ad_hoc.recommended_layout: bounded mixed layout: status strip, milestone trend, risk/action table, owner/open-question block
project_ad_hoc.selector_filter_behavior: only filters required for project scope; avoid general-purpose exploration
project_ad_hoc.navigation_relations: links to source plan, owner, issue/action registry, and replacement dashboard if promoted
project_ad_hoc.expected_interactivity: filter by owner/status, sort action table, link to source artifacts
